history.text: Arab Kingdom of Syria ruled or strongly influenced the territory before the modern state.
State of Syria under French mandate placed the territory under outside administration before modern independence.
First Syrian Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
End of the French mandate placed the territory under outside administration before modern independence.
Part of the United Arab Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
Ba'athist Syria appears in Syria's historical record.
Syrian transitional government was established, giving Syria a new political framework.

worthSeeing.title: Palmyra (Tadmor):
worthSeeing.text: Ancient caravan oasis city and magnificent Roman ruins in the Syrian desert.

worthSeeing.title: Damascus Old City:
worthSeeing.text: UNESCO-listed one of the world's oldest capitals, with the Umayyad Mosque and grand souqs.

worthSeeing.title: Krak des Chevaliers:
worthSeeing.text: One of the world's best-preserved Crusader castles, a UNESCO masterpiece.
